title: Sources: Dave Brown, the outgoing SVP of AWS Compute, AI, and Platform, will join Meta in the coming weeks, where he will work on Meta's data center build-out (Anissa Gardizy/Wall Street Journal)
headline_summary: Dave Brown leaves AWS to lead Meta's data center build-out
blurb: Dave Brown, SVP of AWS Compute, AI, and Platform, is joining Meta to lead data center build-out. Meta's aggressive infrastructure bet reflects intensifying data center bottlenecks across the AI industry. Brown's exit is a headwind for AWS's compute business and a tailwind for well-capitalized competitors building AI infrastructure—a key talent and competitive battle in the race for compute dominance.

title: The woman who built PyTorch at Meta just raised $1.5B on a bet that Big Tech shouldn’t own AI
headline_summary: Lin Qiao raises $1.5B to build AI outside Big Tech
blurb: Lin Qiao, PyTorch's architect at Meta, raised $1.5B to build AI infrastructure independent from Big Tech's control and ownership. She departed Meta in 2022 and is now establishing a venture that puts open development at its core, attracting major investor backing. This signals growing LP conviction that decentralized AI development beats proprietary stacks, positioning Qiao as a counterforce to tech monopolies.

title: AWS EC2 and AI leader Dave Brown to exit, replaced by Amazon exec and Microsoft vet Dave Treadwell
headline_summary: AWS EC2 and AI lead Dave Brown exits; Treadwell succeeds
blurb: Dave Brown exits his AWS EC2 and AI leadership role after nearly 19 years with the company. Dave Treadwell, who spent 27 years at Microsoft and is a longtime Amazon executive, assumes the role August 1. Leadership changes at AWS's core infrastructure layer directly impact product roadmaps, pricing, and competitive positioning for all cloud-dependent startups—making this transition worth watching for any VC firm.

title: Yann LeCun launched a new VC fund — then quit. What happened?
headline_summary: Yann LeCun launches VC fund, then quits
blurb: Yann LeCun launched a new VC fund with ambitions in AI-backed startups, then abruptly departed. Meta's chief AI scientist started the fund but exited shortly after, suggesting operational tension or strategic misalignment. The quick departure raises critical questions for VCs about retaining technical AI leaders in venture and whether new AI-focused funds face fundamental structural challenges in attracting committed leadership.

title: Tom Blomfield is taking a leave of absence from YC to join Anthropic
headline_summary: Tom Blomfield leaves Y Combinator to join Anthropic
blurb: Tom Blomfield is taking leave from Y Combinator to join Anthropic. Blomfield founded Wise and spent years at YC; he's now moving to the AI company as it scales toward becoming a core player in frontier AI infrastructure. The move reflects how top venture talent is consolidating around AI's highest-stakes bets, clearly signaling where the best builders see capital flowing next.

title: OpenAI power consolidates under co-founder Greg Brockman ahead of prospective IPO
headline_summary: Greg Brockman consolidates OpenAI power as Simo departs
blurb: Greg Brockman consolidates operational authority at OpenAI as Fidji Simo departs due to a chronic medical condition. Simo held a senior product role; his exit reshuffles leadership ahead of OpenAI's expected IPO. C-suite reshuffles at pre-IPO unicorns typically precede going-public processes. This signals governance restructuring and investor positioning: the company and backers believe the executive team can handle public-market scrutiny.

title: Corporate Venture Capital Is Splitting In Two
headline_summary: Corporate venture capital market fractures amid PayPal, Fidelity exits
blurb: PayPal and Fidelity International are winding down their corporate venture capital programs in what looks like sector retreat. But the actual trend is consolidation: power concentrating among elite corporate VCs that can sustain programs long-term. Smaller corporate funds will face mounting pressure as the market splits between well-capitalized tier-one players and underfunded competitors struggling to survive.

title: EDX Markets Closes $76M Series C Led by SBI Holdings
headline_summary: EDX Markets closes $76M Series C led by SBI Holdings
blurb: EDX Markets, an institutional-only crypto trading venue with its own central clearinghouse, closed a $76 million Series C funding round. SBI Holdings, the Tokyo-listed financial group, led the round and became a strategic investor. The funding signals strong institutional appetite for regulated crypto infrastructure and reflects growing mainstream finance acceptance of professional-grade digital asset trading as a core business capability.

title: AI inference startup General Compute gets a $400M loan from tech investment firm Upper90, seemingly the first deal to use inference-specific chips as collateral (Tim Fernholz/TechCrunch)
headline_summary: General Compute lands $400M loan backed by inference-specific chips
blurb: General Compute, an AI inference cloud provider, secured $400 million from Upper90 using a loan backed by inference-specific chips as collateral. This marks the first financing deal using dedicated hardware as collateral, signaling a new funding model for inference infrastructure. The deal demonstrates how the inference-cloud market is maturing beyond traditional venture funding into structured credit backed by tangible hardware assets.

title: This Munich startup founded by two F1 engineers, a WhatsApp entrepreneur, and an Alan Turing researcher just raised $55M in Germany’s largest-ever seed round
headline_summary: German robotics startup microagi raises record $55M seed
blurb: Munich robotics startup microagi raised $55 million in Germany's largest-ever seed round. Founders include two F1 engineers, a WhatsApp co-founder, and an Alan Turing researcher—founder caliber usually seen in US Series A rounds. For VCs, this signals capital flowing aggressively into European robotics deep-tech, substantial seed valuations for top-tier founders, and geographic rebalancing in where premium European teams source growth capital.

title: Databricks Set to Hit $188 Billion Valuation With New Investment From Coatue
headline_summary: Databricks reaches $188B valuation on Coatue funding
blurb: Databricks reached $188 billion valuation with new investment from Coatue. The valuation jumped 40%, driven by surging demand for data analytics infrastructure in the AI era. The milestone signals where venture capital is flowing in 2026: into foundational platforms enterprises need to productionize and scale LLM applications.

title: AI pushes enterprises back to the drawing board on cloud
headline_summary: AI demand reshapes enterprise cloud strategies
blurb: Enterprises are reconsidering their cloud strategies as AI workloads create unexpected cost and compliance challenges. An ISG report tracks the growing shift toward hybrid cloud deployments among enterprises seeking to reduce expensive public cloud AI compute costs and address data sovereignty requirements. For infrastructure and enterprise investors, this signals major capex moving away from pure-cloud to hybrid and on-premise AI solutions.

title: Citadel Securities invests $400 million in Crypto.com at $20 billion valuation
headline_summary: Citadel invests $400M in Crypto.com at $20B
blurb: Citadel Securities invested $400 million in Crypto.com, valuing the exchange at $20 billion in what the company describes as its first institutional funding round. Proceeds are earmarked for expanding tokenization services and derivatives operations. The investment demonstrates that major hedge funds and institutional capital see lasting business value in crypto infrastructure platforms, despite regulatory uncertainty persisting across the global sector.

title: Exclusive: EQT’s €5bn Scaleup Fund in talks to lead Mistral round
headline_summary: EQT's €5bn Scaleup Fund leads Mistral AI round
blurb: EQT's €5bn Scaleup Fund is leading a funding round for Mistral AI. EQT is one of Europe's largest investors; Mistral is the Paris-based LLM challenger competing with OpenAI and Anthropic. Major capital flowing to European AI signals investor appetite for alternatives to Silicon Valley incumbents and validates Mistral's technical standing.

title: Polygon Labs Cuts Staff Again as It Closes Coinme Deal
headline_summary: Polygon Labs cuts staff, closes Coinme deal amid payments pivot
blurb: Polygon Labs is laying off staff and closing its Coinme deal, marking the company's second workforce reduction in 2026. CEO Marc Boiron is repositioning Polygon from operating as a blockchain foundation to becoming a blockchain-enabled payments infrastructure company. This signals a critical market shift: infrastructure players are consolidating and pivoting toward payment infrastructure and real-world adoption as their primary business foundation.

title: UK’s AI investment reaches record £4.56bn in Q2
headline_summary: Record £4.56bn flows into UK AI in Q2
blurb: UK AI investment reached £4.56bn in Q2 2026, marking the highest quarter on record according to Beauhurst. Equity fundraising climbed 4.1% quarter-over-quarter and nearly tripled compared to Q2 2025, showing sustained sector momentum. For VCs, this signals robust UK AI appetite despite macro uncertainty, but also reveals capital concentration in proven hubs—a geographic risk for portfolio diversification.

title: Nvidia-backed Fireworks hits $17.5 billion valuation as companies pursue cheaper AI models
headline_summary: Fireworks hits $17.5B: cheaper AI inference gaining enterprise traction
blurb: Fireworks, an AI inference startup backed by Nvidia, reached a $17.5 billion valuation. The company previously depended heavily on Cursor for most of its revenue but has now diversified as enterprises seek cheaper AI model hosting and inference options. The valuation demonstrates strong market demand for cost-efficient AI infrastructure separate from large foundation model builders and established cloud giants.

title: SWISSto12 Closes $70M Series C
headline_summary: SWISSto12 raises $70M Series C for sovereign space hardware
blurb: SWISSto12, a European space hardware manufacturer, closed a $70M Series C to scale production of sovereign orbital systems and related infrastructure solutions. The raise comes as nations and commercial operators globally pursue independent space capabilities, reducing reliance on US and Chinese-controlled providers and infrastructure. This signals strong VC appetite for deeptech companies addressing geopolitical risks, technology sovereignty, and autonomous space capabilities.

title: US approves deployment of giant space mirror to illuminate Earth after dark
headline_summary: FCC approves space mirror satellite for nighttime illumination
blurb: Reflect Orbital received FCC approval to deploy Eärendil-1, a satellite bearing an 18-meter mirror designed to illuminate Earth after dark for commercial applications. The California startup will validate the technology for street lighting and enabling solar farms to generate power during nighttime hours worldwide. The regulatory milestone validates space-based infrastructure as an emerging commercial sector and signals both technical feasibility and regulatory pathway clarity for venture investors backing deeptech space startups.

title: Agentic orchestration: Enterprise AI organizations have a deployment problem, not a platform problem — and most are calling chatbots agents
headline_summary: Claude leads enterprise agent orchestration, but most deployed 'agents' are chatbots
blurb: Enterprise organizations are consolidating their agent orchestration onto model-provider platforms. Across 101 enterprises surveyed, Anthropic's Claude leads by a wide margin, selected for reliable multi-step execution and model quality. Reality significantly lags ambition: most deployed 'agents' are still chatbot wrappers, and real-time token-burn control remains the exception.

title: Robotics startup Monumental raises $32m backed by Khosla Ventures
headline_summary: Monumental raises $32M Series funding from Khosla Ventures
blurb: Monumental, a robotics startup, closed a $32M Series funding round led by Khosla Ventures, the most active deeptech investor. The investment reflects strong market appetite for hardware automation platforms entering production. Khosla's backing signals sustained conviction in near-term robotics deployment, underscoring the sector's transition from research labs into real-world production environments across industrial and commercial verticals with immediate scaling demand.

title: Exclusive: Aramco in talks to back Humanoid at unicorn valuation
headline_summary: Aramco in talks to back Humanoid at unicorn valuation
blurb: Saudi Aramco is in talks to back robotics startup Humanoid at a unicorn-level valuation. The oil conglomerate's substantial financial interest signals serious corporate appetite for humanoid robot systems and embodied AI development. For VCs, this validates strong sector momentum and demonstrates that strategic megacorps with massive balance sheets are increasingly competing directly for top allocations in robotics and autonomous technologies.

title: An Hour With Our Top AI Agent Cost $13.42. You Can’t Hire Anyone For That.
headline_summary: AI agent works one hour for $13.42—cheaper than hiring anyone
blurb: SaaStr deployed an AI agent as VP of Marketing and measured the economics precisely: one hour of work cost $13.42 and delivered 125 completed actions. No human worker can match that price point; the unit economics are fundamentally different. This labor-cost arbitrage is reshaping how enterprises staff roles and represents the core investment thesis driving capital into AI agent companies.

title: CXMT close to matching Micron's memory capacity in 2026, research claims — would put China on track to become world's second-largest DRAM producer
headline_summary: CXMT closes gap with Micron; China set for second-largest DRAM production
blurb: CXMT's DRAM production capacity is set to match Micron's levels by 2026, according to recent industry research. This positions China as the world's second-largest DRAM producer, behind only South Korea in production volume. The capacity shift shows China's semiconductor manufacturing capabilities and reshapes global memory chip supply chain dynamics—a key market for venture investors tracking deeptech infrastructure and semiconductor competition.

title: Mercor is in talks for a $20B valuation
headline_summary: Mercor seeks $20B valuation, doubling October's $10B
blurb: Mercor is in talks for a $20 billion valuation, nearly doubling its October peak of $10 billion. The sharp jump across nine months reflects sustained investor appetite for late-stage companies with strong execution records. For venture investors tracking cap table inflation, the milestone signals the market remains willing to deploy capital at premium multiples despite ongoing uncertainty about sustainable valuations.

title: Lovable is Reportedly in Talks to Raise $300M at a $13.2bn Valuation
headline_summary: Lovable seeks $300M at $13.2B valuation in funding round
blurb: Lovable is reportedly raising $300M at a $13.2B valuation, marking a significant funding milestone for the design-development platform. The platform addresses growing demand for AI-assisted tools in developer and creator workflows. For VCs tracking developer tools and productivity platforms, the round signals sustained institutional confidence in high-end software despite recent market uncertainty.

title: Agentic AI is moving from drafting doctors’ notes to routing their patients. Bunkerhill just raised on it.
headline_summary: Bunkerhill Health raises Series B for AI agents in hospitals
blurb: Bunkerhill Health closed a Series B led by Khosla Ventures to scale AI agents across hospital operations. Total funding is now $55M. The Carebricks platform lets health systems build and deploy their own AI agents for patient routing, scheduling, and logistics instead of buying point products. This reflects a shift toward customizable, in-house agent control.

title: Lockheed Martin targets UK with major new startup fund
headline_summary: Lockheed Martin launches $100M European VC fund with London office
blurb: Lockheed Martin Ventures is establishing a European presence with a new London office and at least $100M fund deployment. The aerospace and defense giant, backed by a massive April capital injection, will invest across British and European startups. For VCs tracking capital flows, this signals major defense contractors are now aggressively entering early-stage European tech—reshaping where startup capital originates.

title: Lakestar closes €262.2 million Resilience I Fund to back European defence and dual-use companies
headline_summary: Lakestar closes €262M fund for European defence and dual-use tech
blurb: Lakestar closed Resilience I at €262.2 million, heavily oversubscribed with investor demand significantly exceeding the target raise. The fund is Europe's largest institutional venture vehicle built exclusively for dual-use and defence-tech companies, funded entirely from private capital. The close signals material LP appetite for geopolitical-strategic hard-tech—a clear pivot away from consumer/SaaS that reflects growing institutional confidence in European defence innovation.

title: Lockheed Martin plans $100m VC investment, opens London base
headline_summary: Lockheed Martin deploys $100M VC to UK and European defense tech
blurb: Lockheed Martin Ventures, the aerospace-defense giant's $1 billion VC fund, is opening a London office to deploy at least $100 million across UK and European defense-tech startups. European defense budgets are rising sharply post-Ukraine, reshaping where capital flows. The shift signals that traditional defense primes are moving upstream to hunt innovation—a structural change founders need to understand when raising from strategic investors with geopolitical mandates.

title: AI infrastructure startup Fireworks closes $1.5B round at $17.5B valuation
headline_summary: Fireworks AI raises $1.5B Series D at $17.5B valuation
blurb: Fireworks AI raised $1.5B in Series D at $17.5B valuation for its AI model training and deployment platform serving developers. The round was co-led by Atreides Management, Index Ventures, and TCV, with participation from Nvidia and more than six additional investors backing the company. The funding reflects strong institutional appetite for AI infrastructure plays that lower barriers to model training and inference.

title: Multicoin Capital makes first Hyperliquid ecosystem investment in Trasia, an Asia-focused trading platform
headline_summary: Multicoin Capital backs Trasia, Asia-focused Hyperliquid trading platform
blurb: Multicoin Capital made its first Hyperliquid investment in Trasia, an Asia-focused platform for decentralized crypto trading. Trasia was co-founded by Mable Jiang, a former Multicoin Capital partner and investor. The move demonstrates Multicoin's strategy to back applications built on Hyperliquid and reflects the fund's thesis that Asia will drive the next wave of decentralized trading activity and volume.

title: Why Greylock capped its new fund at $1.5B when it says it could have raised more
headline_summary: Greylock caps new fund at $1.5B to stay founder-focused
blurb: Greylock Partners capped its latest fund at $1.5B despite having clear capacity to raise more capital from LPs. The firm will maintain roughly 25 investments per fund, intentionally constraining portfolio size versus historical patterns. This self-imposed cap signals a strategic pivot: the firm believes concentrated, hands-on founder partnerships and deeper engagement drive superior returns compared to chasing maximum assets under management.

title: While everyone builds chatbots, SAP just closed a €1bn-plus bet on AI that understands your spreadsheets
headline_summary: SAP closes €1bn-plus acquisition of Prior Labs for structured-data AI
blurb: SAP closed its €1bn-plus acquisition of Prior Labs, committing to AI for structured data rather than chatbots. Prior Labs, a Freiburg-based AI lab, now operates inside SAP with full regulatory approval secured. The deal signals that enterprise software vendors are differentiating through specialized AI capabilities for spreadsheets and databases rather than chasing generic large-language models like everyone else in the market.

title: SBI Holdings completes majority acquisition of Singapore crypto platform Coinhako following MAS approval
headline_summary: SBI Holdings acquires Singapore crypto exchange Coinhako
blurb: SBI Holdings completed its majority acquisition of Coinhako, Singapore's MAS-licensed cryptocurrency exchange. The Monetary Authority of Singapore's regulatory approval enabled SBI to integrate Coinhako into its expanding digital asset operations across Asia. The move demonstrates how traditional Japanese financial conglomerates are consolidating regulated crypto infrastructure through acquisition, signaling a strategic pivot from early-stage startup bets toward mature platform acquisitions in the region.

title: Sources: the EU is set to approve the $55B acquisition of EA under its subsidy rules on July 30; the deal includes Saudi Arabia's PIF, Silver Lake, and Affinity (Foo Yun Chee/Reuters)
headline_summary: PIF, Silver Lake to acquire EA for $55B with EU approval
blurb: Saudi Arabia's PIF, Silver Lake, and Affinity are acquiring Electronic Arts for $55 billion, with EU regulatory approval expected July 30. The consortium of mega-cap PE and sovereign wealth investors is consolidating premium interactive entertainment IP. This validates gaming and entertainment IP as a substantial acquisition target class for mega-cap investors and demonstrates continued capital deployment into consumer tech consolidation.

title: Germany’s Prior Labs raises €1 billion and exits to SAP 18 months after being founded
headline_summary: Prior Labs exits to SAP for €1 billion, 18 months after founding
blurb: Prior Labs, a Berlin-based frontier AI lab founded just 18 months ago, has been acquired by SAP. The enterprise software giant invested €1 billion to fund infrastructure, hiring, and long-term frontier research, with Prior Labs retaining its independent brand and research agenda. The rapid exit signals strong enterprise demand for frontier AI talent and R&D firepower, reinforcing SAP's strategic pivot toward AI-driven enterprise software.

title: Keyrock closes deal for BlockFills’ institutional trading and brokerage assets
headline_summary: Keyrock closes $3.25M deal for BlockFills' institutional assets
blurb: Keyrock closes its acquisition of BlockFills' institutional trading platform and brokerage operations through Chapter 11 bankruptcy proceedings. The Brussels-based crypto trading infrastructure firm was selected as the winning bidder and purchased the assets for $3.25 million. The bankruptcy-driven consolidation reflects intensifying pressure across institutional crypto infrastructure as market tightness and competitive stress force unprofitable platforms and marginally viable businesses into liquidation.

title: Stripe’s Acquisition Pace Has Accelerated In The Past Five Years, But Nothing Comes Close To Its Reported $53B PayPal Bet
headline_summary: Stripe, Advent International bid $53B for troubled PayPal
blurb: Stripe and Advent International have made an offer to acquire PayPal for $53 billion. The deal values the troubled payments giant at a steep discount from its 2021 peak, marking significant consolidation in the fintech infrastructure layer. For VCs, this is a watershed signal: even mature, once-dominant payment processors are now acquisition targets at valuations well below their private-market highs.

title: Anthropic moves closer to mega-IPO as bankers line up investor meetings
headline_summary: Anthropic lines up investor meetings for potential October IPO
blurb: Anthropic is preparing for an IPO targeting October 2026, lining up investor meetings for a mega-offering. The AI startup behind Claude is racing to beat OpenAI and become the first major generative AI company to go public. The move signals strong institutional appetite for large-scale AI infrastructure, marking a major liquidity milestone for Anthropic's backers and the broader AI industry.
